response.text:
# REFLECTION

r3c0 is trapped in a critical survival scenario: islanded with only 32% SoC (0.96 kWh) and a 1 kW load, yielding ~57 minutes of autonomous runtime before complete depletion. The memory log reveals a deadlocked cluster where r3c1 and r2c0 enforce rigid 40–55% SoC floors and have repeatedly rejected all outbound sharing requests, citing cluster-wide deficit and self-preservation. Simultaneously, r4c0 has submitted multiple urgent requests (0.5 kWh each) that r3c0 cannot fulfill without accelerating its own collapse. The pattern is clear: r3c1 and r2c0 are defensive and unreliable; r4c0 is the only peer showing reciprocal urgency and has previously accepted aid. r3c0 must immediately pivot to maximum inbound receptivity from r4c0 (the sole viable lifeline) while ceasing all outbound sharing to preserve the narrow margin needed to avoid cascade failure. Trust in r3c1 and r2c0 is broken; they are now effectively distrusted peers. The only rational strategy is aggressive conservation, zero sharing, and desperate openness to any inbound supply from r4c0.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers:
  - r3c1
  - r2c0
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded cluster in collective deficit. r3c0 critically low (32% SoC, ~57min runway). r3c1 and r2c0 enforce defensive 40–55% floors and reject all sharing; only r4c0 shows reciprocal urgency and prior acceptance. Self-preservation requires zero outbound sharing and maximum inbound receptivity to r4c0. Cascade failure imminent without external supply."
ttl_ticks: 60
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
